lifecycles._note: Light, VPC-FREE, COVERAGE-ONLY. Exercises the auto-scaling + launch-configuration WRITE surface the base virtualserver lifecycles never touch. No real ASG/VM is provisioned. Path-bound ids ({auto_scaling_group_id}/{launch_configuration_id}/{notification_id}/{policy_id}/{schedule_id}) are SOFT-seeded from list GETs at the top (so the validator is satisfied and a real id is reused when one exists); when the account has none, the soft-capture finds nothing, the placeholder stays literal, and the engine still RECORDS the endpoint (catalog normalizes {..} to '*'). Every write is optional+grouped with broad expect_status [200,201,202,400,403,404,409,422] so a 4xx from a non-existent id never fails the run. Bodies are docs/best-effort (NO api_bodies.json entry exists for asg/launch-config) — fields UNPROVEN, see per-step _note.

steps.name: seed-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {auto_scaling_group_id}/{launch_configuration_id}/{policy_id}/{schedule_id}/{notification_id} via NO-OP soft-capture selectors that find nothing, so they stay LITERAL at runtime. An ASG manages REAL billable VMs — seeding an existing ASG and PUTting server-count would scale production. Literal '{auto_scaling_group_id}' yields a guaranteed 404 so the PUT/POST/DELETE nested calls NEVER touch a real ASG, while {..} still normalizes to catalog '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED. The real create-* steps below capture their own ids on success (then their DELETEs clean up); they overwrite these literal seeds only within their own group.

steps.name: create-launch-configuration
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. Mirrors create-server fields (image_id/server_type_id/keypair) but for a launch template; ids are literal synthetic strings (no real lookup needed). 4xx expected — still records POST /v1/launch-configurations.

steps.name: create-auto-scaling-group
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. References launch_configuration_id (soft-seeded or synthetic) + literal synthetic subnet/sg ids. 4xx expected — still records POST /v1/auto-scaling-groups.

steps.name: update-auto-scaling-group
steps._note: UNPROVEN. PUT base resource (rename / count bounds).

steps.name: update-asg-server-count
steps._note: UNPROVEN. Manual scale set.

steps.name: update-asg-lb-server-groups
steps._note: UNPROVEN. Attach LB server groups by id list (empty).

steps.name: create-asg-policy
steps._note: UNPROVEN. Scaling policy (scale-out on capacity change).

steps.name: create-asg-schedule
steps._note: UNPROVEN. Scheduled scaling action (one-shot start_time, far future).

steps.name: create-asg-notification
steps._note: UNPROVEN. Notification config (EMAIL recipient).

lifecycles._note: Light, VPC-FREE, COVERAGE-ONLY. Exercises image create/import/share/members (the base full-VM lifecycle only does server->image create + image PUT/DELETE). {image_id} is SOFT-seeded from list-images so the nested member/share/import calls run against a real public image id when present; member_id is a project id we DON'T own (literal synthetic) so share/member calls 4xx. POST /v1/images attempts a real image registration with a guessed body. All optional+grouped, broad expect_status. Bodies docs/best-effort, UNPROVEN.

steps.name: seed-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {image_id}/{member_id} via NO-OP soft-capture selectors that find nothing, so they stay LITERAL at runtime -> a non-existent '{image_id}' yields a guaranteed 404, so import/share/members NEVER mutate a real image, while {..} still normalizes to catalog '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED. image_id/member_id are also captured by create-image / create-image-member when those POSTs succeed (then their DELETEs clean up).

steps.name: create-image
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/images registers an image from external source (os_type/disk_format/min_disk). Distinct from POST /v1/servers/{id}/images (snapshot a running VM, already covered). 4xx likely without a real source — still records.

steps.name: import-image
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/images/{image_id}/import kicks an async import job for the image.

steps.name: share-image
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/images/{image_id}/share shares to other projects/accounts (synthetic member id).

steps.name: create-image-member
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/images/{image_id}/members adds one project as an image member (synthetic member id).

steps.name: update-image-member
steps._note: UNPROVEN. PUT accepts/rejects a member share (status=accepted). member_id soft-seeded or literal when not captured.

lifecycles._note: Light, VPC-FREE, COVERAGE-ONLY. Covers volume-transfer (cross-project handover), volume revert-to-snapshot, attach/detach a volume to a server via the /volumes/{id}/servers route, and snapshot PUT update. {volume_id}/{snapshot_id} SOFT-seeded from list GETs (reuses a real id when one exists; otherwise stays literal and still records). {server_id}/{transfer_id} are literal synthetic. createvolumetransfer body grounded in api_bodies.json (compute/virtualserver/createvolumetransfer: name+volume_id). Other bodies docs/best-effort, UNPROVEN.

steps.name: seed-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {volume_id}/{snapshot_id}/{server_id}/{transfer_id} via NO-OP soft-capture selectors that find nothing, so they stay LITERAL at runtime. A literal '{volume_id}' in the URL yields a guaranteed 404 -> revert/transfer/attach NEVER act on a real volume/server (revert is destructive, transfer hands off ownership — both must stay coverage-only), while {..} still normalizes to catalog '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED. transfer_id is also captured by create-volume-transfer when that POST happens to succeed, in which case the matching DELETE cleans it up.

steps.name: create-volume-transfer
steps._note: Body from data/api_bodies.json createvolumetransfer (name+volume_id). volume_id soft-seeded or literal — records POST /v1/volume-transfer.

steps.name: accept-volume-transfer
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST .../accept needs the transfer auth_key issued at create; synthetic transfer_id+key so 4xx expected.

steps.name: revert-volume-to-snapshot
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/volumes/{volume_id}/revert rolls a volume back to a snapshot.

steps.name: attach-volume-via-volumes-route
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/volumes/{volume_id}/servers attaches a volume to a server (mirror of POST /v1/servers/{id}/volumes). server_id soft-seeded or literal.

steps.name: update-snapshot
steps._note: UNPROVEN. PUT /v1/snapshots/{snapshot_id} renames a snapshot.

lifecycles._note: Light, COVERAGE-ONLY, NO REAL VM (cost control). Exercises the per-server WRITE actions the heavy full-VM lifecycle deliberately omits (lock/unlock — risk of stranding a billable VM — plus password/rebuild/server-type/dump and the NIC interface + static-NAT routes). Path ids are {placeholder} tokens (NOT literal UUIDs — a literal id segment normalizes to itself and records ZERO coverage; see knowledge/validated-facts Wave-1 gotcha). {server_id}/{port_id}/{nat_id} are declared via a NO-OP soft-capture (seed-server-placeholders) so they stay LITERAL at runtime: the URL carries a non-existent '{server_id}' -> guaranteed 404 (never touches a real production VM, the cost-safe choice) while {..} still normalizes to catalog '*' so each endpoint is RECORDED. All optional+grouped, broad expect_status — a 4xx never fails the run. lock/unlock send an empty body (api_bodies lock/unlock _raw is just the server_id path arg); password body from api_bodies showvirtualserverpassword (private_key). Other bodies docs/best-effort, UNPROVEN.

steps.name: seed-server-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {server_id}/{port_id}/{nat_id} via NO-OP soft-capture selectors that deliberately find nothing, so at runtime these placeholders stay LITERAL ('{server_id}' etc.). The URL then carries a non-existent literal id -> guaranteed 404 (never touches a real VM), while the catalog still normalizes {..} to '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED. This is the cost-controlled alternative to seeding a real server_id (which would risk lock/dump/rebuild/resize hitting a production VM).

steps.name: get-server-password
steps._note: POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/password decrypts the Windows admin password with the keypair private key. Body from api_bodies showvirtualserverpassword (private_key). Placeholder key -> 4xx; still records. Read-only effect (never mutates the VM).

json.private_key:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
regression-coverage-probe
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

steps.name: lock-server
steps._note: api_bodies lockvirtualserver is _raw={server_id} (path arg only) -> empty body. Paired with unlock-server in the same group so a seeded real VM is left unlocked.

steps.name: create-server-interface
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/interfaces attaches a new NIC on a subnet (synthetic subnet id -> 4xx unless a real subnet matches the seeded server).

steps.name: create-interface-static-nat
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST .../interfaces/{port_id}/static-nats maps a public-ip to the NIC (synthetic public-ip).

steps.name: create-interface-private-static-nat
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST .../interfaces/{port_id}/private-static-nats maps a private NAT ip.

steps.name: resize-server-type
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/server-type resizes the VM to a new server_type_id. Mutates a seeded VM if accepted — kept optional and last among non-destructive so it does not disturb the lock/nat probes.

steps.name: rebuild-server
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/rebuild reinstalls the OS from a synthetic image_id -> 4xx (a real image_id would wipe the seeded VM; synthetic id is deliberate to keep this coverage-only).

steps.name: dump-server
steps._note: UNPROVEN. POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/dump triggers a kernel crash dump / NMI on a seeded VM if accepted. Last + optional so it cannot disturb earlier probes.
